title: Invention patents granted annually in the United States since 1840
caption:
Invention patents granted annually in the United States since 1840

This stacked area chart tracks the annual volume of invention patents granted in the United States from 1840 through 2014, categorized by technological field. The vertical axis measures the count of patents, ranging from 0 to 300,000, while the horizontal axis marks time in 20-year increments. From 1840 until approximately 1940, the total number of patents granted annually remains relatively low, generally staying below the 50,000 mark, with visible contributions primarily from the Mechanical, Chemical, and Other categories. A distinct upward trend begins in the mid-20th century, with the total count surpassing 50,000 around 1960 and reaching approximately 100,000 by 1980. The most significant expansion occurs after the year 2000, where the total volume rises sharply, peaking near the 300,000 level by 2014. This recent surge is driven largely by the Computers & communications category, which forms the largest segment at the top of the stack in the final years, followed by substantial layers for Electronics and Medical patents. The Mechanical and Chemical categories maintain a presence throughout the timeline but represent a smaller proportion of the total volume in the most recent decade compared to the digital and electronic sectors. The Other category forms the base layer, showing a gradual increase over the full period but remaining the smallest or second-smallest segment in the later years.
